macro_segments.title: Four Brothers and the Concern of Losing the Opportunity for Yibum or Chalitzah

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishnah: Four Brothers Where Each Must Do Chalitzah
Alternative Explanation: The Problem of Bittul Mitzvat Yabam (Nullifying the Yibum Obligation)
Gemara's Challenge: Why Four Brothers Rather Than Three?
The Chiddush of Four Brothers: Stronger Zikah Makes the Problem More Pronounced
Resolution: We Are Only Concerned That One Brother Will Die, Not Two

macro_segments.title: Three Sisters and Two Brothers: The Principle of Chalitzah Psula (Defective Chalitzah)

micro_segments.title: Setup: Three Sisters Fall Before Two Brothers (Yissachar and Zebulun)
Introduction to Chalitzah Psula: Defective Chalitzah Requires All Brothers
Sequential Deaths and the Strength of Chalitzah: Complete vs. Weakened
Rachel as Achot Zikukato (Sister of Zikah) and Sarah's Status Between Brothers

macro_segments.title: Rav vs. Shmuel: The Debate Over Chalitzah Psula and Multiple Brothers

micro_segments.title: Rav's Position: Chalitzah Psula Requires Chalitzah from All Brothers
Shmuel's Position: One Brother Is Sufficient Even for Weakened Chalitzah
Gemara's Challenge to Shmuel: Doesn't Shmuel Himself Hold Chalitzah Me'alya (Superior Chalitzah) Is Needed?

macro_segments.title: The Case of Co-Wives and the Strength of Chalitzah: Which Wife Should Receive Chalitzah?

micro_segments.title: Shmuel: Chalitzah with the Weaker Woman Does Not Free the Co-Wife
Shmuel: Chalitzah with the Stronger Woman (Co-Wife) Frees the Sister
The Principle: Achot Zikukato Creates a Second-Rate Chalitzah Status

macro_segments.title: Reinterpretation of Shmuel: Sequential vs. Simultaneous Deaths and the Order of Chalitzah

micro_segments.title: If All Three Men Died Simultaneously, All Women Would Have Equal Status
When Deaths Are Sequential, Each Chalitzah Changes the Status of Remaining Women
Rachel as Achot Chalutzato After Chalitzah Is Done to Leah

macro_segments.title: Shmuel's Radical Reread: The Asur Problem of Tzaras Krovat Chalitzato and the Order of Beginning

micro_segments.title: The Prohibition Against Marrying Tzaras Krovat Chalitzato (Co-Wife of Chalitzah Relative)
Shmuel's Reread: If He Begins with Sisters, He Cannot Finish with Co-Wives
If He Begins with Co-Wives, He Can Finish with Sisters

macro_segments.title: Rav Ashi's Simple Explanation and the Nature of Zikah Strength

micro_segments.title: Rav Ashi: The Zikah Between Sisters Is Not Strong Enough to Create Serious Co-Wife Problems
Application: Chalitzah with the Co-Wife Still Frees the Sister

macro_segments.title: Alternative Explanation: Shmuel as Following Beit Shammai on Tzaros (Co-Wives)

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Abba bar Mammel: Maybe Shmuel Follows Beit Shammai, Who Permit Tzaros
Covering Both Viewpoints: Establishing a Takanah That Tzaros Must Receive Chalitzah
Resolution: We Follow the Stringent View and Require Chalitzah for Co-Wives
